github_issue_comment_templates.overview: Templates for GitHub issue comments when Bob detects a false positive CVE. These comments provide detailed analysis and guide the DevOps engineer through the confirmation process.

false_positive_comment_template.title: Standard False Positive Analysis Comment

usage_instructions.instruction: Include specific file paths and line numbers in evidence
Add code snippets to support your analysis

template.name: transitive_dependency
template.title: False Positive: Transitive Dependency
template.content:
## 🔍 False Positive Analysis: Transitive Dependency
### 📋 Summary
**CVE**: {CVE-ID} **Package**: `{package-name}` {version} **Determination**: **FALSE POSITIVE** - Transitive dependency not directly used
### 🔬 Analysis
This package is a **transitive dependency** (dependency of a dependency) and is never directly imported or used in our application code.
#### Dependency Chain ``` {direct-dependency} → {intermediate-dependency} → {vulnerable-package} ```
#### Code Search Results - ✅ **Direct imports**: 0 found - ✅ **Indirect usage**: 0 found - ✅ **Configuration references**: 0 found
**Search performed:** ```bash search_files: regex: (import {package}|from {package}|require.*{package}) path: . file_pattern: *.{ext} ```
**Result**: No matches found in any application code.
### ✅ Recommendation
**No action required.** The vulnerable package is present in our dependency tree but is never used by our application. The vulnerability cannot be exploited.
### 🎯 Next Steps
@devops-engineer If you agree, please approve the `devops-approval` environment in the workflow run to trigger Concert API update.

template.name: unused_feature
template.title: False Positive: Vulnerable Feature Not Used
template.content:
## 🔍 False Positive Analysis: Vulnerable Feature Not Used
### 📋 Summary
**CVE**: {CVE-ID} **Package**: `{package-name}` {version} **Vulnerable Feature**: `{feature-name}` **Determination**: **FALSE POSITIVE** - Vulnerable feature not used
### 🔬 Analysis
While our application uses `{package-name}`, it does **not use the vulnerable feature** affected by this CVE.
#### Package Usage **Files using {package-name}:** {list-of-files}
**Features used:** {list-of-features-used}
**Vulnerable feature (`{vulnerable-feature}`):** - ❌ Not imported - ❌ Not called - ❌ Not configured
#### Code Evidence
**Example from [{file}]({file}:{line}):** ```{language} {code-snippet-showing-safe-usage} ```
**Analysis**: This code uses {package-name} for {purpose}, but never calls the vulnerable `{vulnerable-function}` function.
### ✅ Recommendation
**No action required.** The vulnerable code path is not reachable in our application.
### 🎯 Next Steps
@devops-engineer If you agree, please approve the `devops-approval` environment in the workflow run to trigger Concert API update.

template.name: mitigated_by_controls
template.title: False Positive: Mitigated by Security Controls
template.content:
## 🔍 False Positive Analysis: Mitigated by Security Controls
### 📋 Summary
**CVE**: {CVE-ID} **Package**: `{package-name}` {version} **Determination**: **FALSE POSITIVE** - Exploitation prevented by security controls
### 🔬 Analysis
While the vulnerable code path exists, **security controls prevent exploitation**.
#### Vulnerability Details - **Type**: {vulnerability-type} - **Attack Vector**: {attack-vector} - **Exploitation Requirement**: {requirement}
#### Security Controls in Place
**1. {Control-Name}** - **Location**: [{file}]({file}:{line}) - **Function**: {what-it-does} - **Effectiveness**: Blocks {attack-type} before reaching vulnerable code
**Code:** ```{language} {security-control-code} ```
**2. {Additional-Control}** {additional-control-details}
#### Attack Path Analysis ``` User Input → Security Control → Validation → Application Code ↓ Blocks malicious input ```
The vulnerable function is only reachable after input passes through security controls that prevent exploitation.
### ✅ Recommendation
**No action required.** Security controls effectively prevent exploitation of this vulnerability.
**Note**: Ensure security controls remain in place. If controls are removed, this CVE should be re-evaluated.
### 🎯 Next Steps
@devops-engineer If you agree, please approve the `devops-approval` environment in the workflow run to trigger Concert API update.

template.name: internal_only_service
template.title: False Positive: Internal-Only Service
template.content:
## 🔍 False Positive Analysis: Internal-Only Service
### 📋 Summary
**CVE**: {CVE-ID} **Package**: `{package-name}` {version} **Determination**: **FALSE POSITIVE** - Service not exposed to untrusted users
### 🔬 Analysis
This service is **internal-only** and not exposed to untrusted users, making exploitation impossible.
#### Deployment Context - **Network**: {internal-network-description} - **Access**: {access-control-description} - **Exposure**: {exposure-level}
#### Security Boundaries ``` Internet → Firewall → VPN → Internal Network → This Service ↓ Blocks external access ```
#### Configuration Evidence
**From [{config-file}]({config-file}):** ```{language} {config-showing-internal-binding} ```
**Analysis**: Service binds to {internal-address}, accessible only from {trusted-network}.
#### Attack Feasibility - ❌ No external network access - ❌ Requires VPN authentication - ❌ Internal network only - ❌ No untrusted user input
### ✅ Recommendation
**No action required.** The deployment context prevents exploitation by untrusted actors.
**Note**: If deployment changes to expose this service externally, this CVE should be re-evaluated.
### 🎯 Next Steps
@devops-engineer If you agree, please approve the `devops-approval` environment in the workflow run to trigger Concert API update.

template.name: configuration_disabled
template.title: False Positive: Vulnerable Feature Disabled
template.content:
## 🔍 False Positive Analysis: Vulnerable Feature Disabled
### 📋 Summary
**CVE**: {CVE-ID} **Package**: `{package-name}` {version} **Vulnerable Feature**: `{feature-name}` **Determination**: **FALSE POSITIVE** - Feature disabled in configuration
### 🔬 Analysis
The vulnerable feature is **explicitly disabled** in our application configuration.
#### Configuration Evidence
**From [{config-file}]({config-file}:{line}):** ```{language} {config-snippet-showing-disabled-feature} ```
**Analysis**: {feature-name} is set to `{disabled-value}`, preventing the vulnerable code path from being executed.
#### Feature Status - ✅ Feature disabled in configuration - ✅ Vulnerable code path unreachable - ✅ No way to enable without configuration change
#### Code Path Analysis ``` Application Start → Load Config → Check {feature-name} ↓ Disabled → Skip vulnerable code ```
### ✅ Recommendation
**No action required.** The vulnerable feature is disabled and cannot be exploited.
**Note**: If configuration changes to enable this feature, this CVE should be re-evaluated.
### 🎯 Next Steps
@devops-engineer If you agree, please approve the `devops-approval` environment in the workflow run to trigger Concert API update.

comment_formatting_guidelines.guideline: Use clear section headers with emojis for visual organization
Include specific file paths as clickable links: [{file}]({file}:{line})
Provide code snippets with proper syntax highlighting
Use checkboxes (✅/❌) for clear yes/no indicators
Include diagrams or flow charts for complex scenarios
Keep total comment length under 2000 characters when possible
Use collapsible sections for very detailed analysis

best_practices.practice: Always provide specific evidence with file paths and line numbers
Explain WHY it's a false positive, not just THAT it is
Include code snippets to support your analysis
Make it easy for engineer to confirm by adding label
Provide context about what would make it a true positive
Note any conditions that could change the determination

example_complete_comment.title: Complete Example: Flask Cookie Parsing CVE
example_complete_comment.comment:
## 🔍 False Positive Analysis
After analyzing the codebase, I've determined this CVE is a **false positive** for our application.
### 📋 Summary
**CVE**: CVE-2023-30861 **Package**: `flask` 2.2.0 **Severity**: HIGH (CVSS 7.5) **Determination**: **FALSE POSITIVE**
**Reason**: Application uses Flask but never uses sessions or cookies, making the cookie parsing vulnerability unexploitable.
---
### 🔬 Detailed Analysis
#### Vulnerability Context - **Type**: Cookie parsing vulnerability - **Vulnerable Function**: `werkzeug.http.parse_cookie()` - **Attack Vector**: Malicious cookie headers can cause denial of service - **Exploitation Requirements**: Application must process user-supplied cookies
#### Code Analysis
**Package Usage in Codebase:**
Flask is imported in 2 files: - [`app/main.py`](app/main.py:1) - [`app/api.py`](app/api.py:1)
**Search performed:** ```bash search_files: regex: (request\.cookies|session\[|parse_cookie) path: . file_pattern: *.py ```
**Result**: 0 matches found
**Vulnerable Function Calls:**
No calls to cookie-related functions found: - ❌ `request.cookies` - Not used - ❌ `session[]` - Not used - ❌ `parse_cookie()` - Not used
**Exploitability Assessment:**
The application uses Flask only for basic routing and JSON responses. It does not: - Process cookies - Use sessions - Parse cookie headers - Accept cookie-based authentication
---
### 📊 Evidence
#### Files Analyzed - [`app/main.py`](app/main.py) - Flask app initialization - [`app/api.py`](app/api.py) - API endpoints
#### Key Findings - ✅ Flask imported but only for routing - ✅ No cookie or session usage - ✅ All endpoints return JSON only - ✅ No authentication using cookies
#### Code Examples
**From [`app/api.py`](app/api.py:10-15):** ```python from flask import Flask, jsonify
app = Flask(__name__)
@app.route('/api/data') def get_data(): return jsonify({'data': 'value'}) ```
**Analysis**: This code uses Flask for basic routing and JSON responses. No cookies or sessions are used, so the cookie parsing vulnerability cannot be exploited.
---
### ✅ Recommendation
**No action required.** The vulnerable cookie parsing code path is not reachable in our application because we don't use cookies or sessions.
---
### 🎯 Next Steps
@devops-engineer Please review this analysis. If you agree this is a false positive:
1. **Approve the `devops-approval` environment** in the workflow run 2. Go to Actions tab → Find the running workflow → Click "Review deployments" 3. The workflow will automatically update Concert API to mark this CVE as false positive 4. The issue will be closed automatically after Concert is updated
**Concert API Update Details:** - **Endpoint**: `PATCH /concert/ingestion/api/v1/vulnerability_assessments` - **Status**: "False positive" - **CVE**: CVE-2023-30861 - **Application**: my-flask-app
---
*🤖 Automated analysis by Bob DevOps Concert Shell V2*
